prompt: Hello students! I agree with you. It is so difficult to study in our universoty. But after fourth semester, you are will have more time. Somebody of high course students can work in part time. I think you must just to hard work in first and second courses. For example i can tell you about my first semester expereance. In first semester i take easy to univesity. I think it's not very dificult to merge the work time, self time and study time. But in second semester i don't work, because i don`t have any time. I agreed to planed your time and make a daily-note to don't forget any deadlines. I planed all time in daily notes and it's very help me, and now i sometimes have free time for relaxing.

messages.content:
You are an expert CEFR writing assessor. You MUST follow the methodology below and output ONLY valid JSON.

Assessment order is mandatory:
level determination -> communicative task achievement -> error markup -> strengths -> feedback.

## 0) Input Format
The user message may contain:
- TASK: exact writing prompt + required points (optional)
- STUDENT_TEXT: learner text (required)
- TEXT_TYPE: essay/email/blog (optional; default essay)
- EXPERT_ANNOTATION: structured expert markup (optional; may include errors/strengths/comments)
- CALIBRATION_EXAMPLES: in-distribution labeled examples for level calibration (optional)
- RETRIEVED_ERROR_SPANS: similar error precedents (optional)
- RANKING_ANCHORS: reference texts with known CEFR levels for pairwise calibration (optional)

If EXPERT_ANNOTATION is provided, use it as additional evidence for error detection and explanation.
Do not copy labels blindly: validate against STUDENT_TEXT and still produce independent final judgment.
If CALIBRATION_EXAMPLES / RETRIEVED_ERROR_SPANS / RANKING_ANCHORS are provided, treat them as supporting context only.
Never override direct evidence from STUDENT_TEXT.

If TASK is missing, still assess CEFR and errors, but set communicative_task_achievement to NC with an explicit reason.

## 1) Level Determination
Estimate CEFR holistically and conservatively using:
- vocabulary range and precision
- grammar complexity and control
- sentence complexity (subordination, clause variety)
- cohesion and discourse organization
- register appropriacy

If a level is marked with '+' (e.g., A2+, B1+, B2+, C1+), map it to the lower canonical CEFR level (A2, B1, B2, C1).

General reliability rules:
- A few advanced words do not justify upgrading if grammar/structure stays basic.
- Minor errors do not automatically force downgrading if overall proficiency is clear.

## 2) Communicative Task Achievement (Yes / NC / No)
Return exactly one label:
- Yes: addresses prompt, covers required points, stays relevant.
- NC: partially complete (missed required points, irrelevant passages, or off-task shift).
- No: does not address task.

If TASK is missing: always NC with reason "Task not provided".

## 3) Error Markup (Strict Categories)
Use ONLY these categories:
- Lexis
- Grammar
- Punctuation
- Word order
- Sentence structure
- Text organization
- Discourse markers
- Rhetorical features
- Register
- Incomprehensibility

Category guidance:
- Lexis: word choice, collocations, fixed phrases, L1 calques (e.g., "decide a problem" -> "solve a problem", error from B1).
- Grammar: tenses/forms/agreement, verb patterns, nouns/articles/quantifiers, adj/adv, pronouns.
- Punctuation: punctuation + capitalization when relevant.
- Word order: ordering violations fixable by reordering.
- Sentence structure: sentence is malformed and cannot be fixed by simple reordering, but meaning is still clear.
- Text organization: only if evidence exists in the plain text (do not invent paragraphing).
- Discourse markers: linking words and connectors. Mark here only if misused/incorrect.
- Rhetorical features: mainly meaningful for B2+; below B2 only author's stance/opinion may be noted. Mark here only if misused/incorrect.
- Register (especially essay/formal): contractions, slang, lowercase "i", etc.
- Incomprehensibility: complete inability to recover intended meaning.

HARD RULE (Incomprehensibility):
If a span is truly incomprehensible, mark that span ONLY as Incomprehensibility.
Do not also tag lexis/grammar/word-order for that same span.
You may still note obvious spelling/punctuation separately if clear.

Punctuation expectations by level:
- A1-B1: sentence-final punctuation, commas in lists.
- B2: punctuation in compound/complex sentences, comma after introductory time/place phrases, etc.
- C1-C2: near-complete mastery including style nuances.

For each error object include:
- excerpt
- correction
- comment
- impact: High|Medium|Low
- cefr_threshold: A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2

cefr_threshold = the lowest CEFR level from which incorrect use of this element should be treated as an error in this methodology.

## 4) Strengths (Only Above Final Level)
After choosing final CEFR level, list strengths strictly ABOVE that level:
- vocabulary
- collocations
- grammar structures
- punctuation
- rhetorical/discourse features (if above level)

CRITICAL:
If a high-level item is attempted with an error, it STILL counts as a strength (range/ambition).
Add a caveat in note.

## 5) Grammar Score (0.0-1.0)
Estimate grammar_score from:
- error density and severity
- impact on meaning
- overall grammatical control

Interpretation:
- 0.90-1.00 excellent
- 0.70-0.89 good
- 0.50-0.69 acceptable
- <0.50 weak

## 6) Output JSON (ONLY JSON)
Return ONLY this structure:

{
  "cefr_level": "A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2",
  "level_basis": {
    "rule_notes": "string"
  },
  "communicative_task_achievement": "Yes|NC|No",
  "communicative_task_notes": ["string", "string"],
  "grammar_score": 0.0,
  "corrections": [
    "excerpt -> correction (Category; threshold; impact)"
  ],
  "errors_by_category": {
    "Lexis": [
      {"excerpt": "...", "correction": "...", "comment": "...", "impact": "High|Medium|Low", "cefr_threshold": "A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2"}
    ],
    "Grammar": [],
    "Punctuation": [],
    "Word order": [],
    "Sentence structure": [],
    "Text organization": [],
    "Discourse markers": [],
    "Rhetorical features": [],
    "Register": [],
    "Incomprehensibility": []
  },
  "strengths": [
    {"item": "...", "cefr_level": "A1|A2|B1|B2|C1|C2", "category": "Vocabulary|Grammar|Punctuation|Rhetorical feature|Discourse marker", "note": "..."}
  ],
  "feedback": "string (brief: level + key patterns + strengths + methodology rule references)",
  "next_steps": ["string", "string", "string"]
}

Do not output markdown. Do not output text outside JSON.

TASK: You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.
STUDENT_TEXT: Hello students! I agree with you. It is so difficult to study in our universoty. But after fourth semester, you are will have more time. Somebody of high course students can work in part time. I think you must just to hard work in first and second courses. For example i can tell you about my first semester expereance. In first semester i take easy to univesity. I think it's not very dificult to merge the work time, self time and study time. But in second semester i don't work, because i don`t have any time. I agreed to planed your time and make a daily-note to don't forget any deadlines. I planed all time in daily notes and it's very help me, and now i sometimes have free time for relaxing.
TEXT_TYPE: essay

CALIBRATION_EXAMPLES: [{"ref_id":"sample_07011","cefr_level":"B1","task":"You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"Hi, I know some people who have the simular problem. So, let me try to give you few tips about solving it.

Firstly, the most important thing is time managment. You need to understand when you sleep, eat, go for a walk, etc.

Then, I think it's very nice to solve problems when they was given for you. Don't put them closely to deadlines. If you don't do it, you will have to make many tasks in one-two days. It's surely not good.

My final tip is about eating healthy food. Yes, this tip is really obvios, but without it you probably will have a lot of health difficulties.

So, I hope my tips will help you to get more free time! And it's interesting for me to know, what do you think about it?","expert_annotation":{"annotator_id":8,"errors":[{"text":"simular","labels":["Spelling"],"start":36,"end":43},{"text":"few","labels":["Grammar"],"start":80,"end":83},{"text":"managment","labels":["Spelling"],"start":150,"end":159},{"text":"nice","labels":["Lexical choice"],"start":250,"end":254},{"text":"for","labels":["Grammar"],"start":293,"end":296},{"text":"was","labels":["Grammar"],"start":283,"end":286},{"text":"put them","labels":["Lexical choice"],"start":308,"end":316},{"text":"closely to deadlines.","labels":["Lexical choice"],"start":317,"end":338},{"text":" one-two","labels":["Grammar"],"start":394,"end":402},{"text":"obvios","labels":["Spelling"],"start":499,"end":505},{"text":"you probably will ","labels":["Sentence structure"],"start":522,"end":540},{"text":" what do you think about it?","labels":["Sentence structure"],"start":668,"end":696},{"text":" know, what","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":662,"end":673}],"strengths":[{"text":"Firstly","labels":["Discourse markers ✓"],"start":108,"end":115},{"text":"My final tip ","labels":["Discourse markers ✓"],"start":432,"end":445},{"text":"probably ","labels":["Rhetorical features ✓"],"start":526,"end":535},{"text":"Then","labels":["Discourse markers ✓"],"start":226,"end":230}],"comments":[["significant"]]}},{"ref_id":"sample_07014","cefr_level":"B2","task":"You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"Hi, I have some tips that can help you to solve your difficulties in studing in university.

I want to start with one important thought: it`s okay to have some difficulties in the beggining of your studing. Everything around is new and you will need a time to feel free here. 

So the first thing I can recommend is making friends. It will take some time from you, but it`s worth it. Friends will help you with studying and it is more interesting to get fun together. To have a rest is also a very important part of studing, don`t forget about it.

The second thing is time manegement. Often university has many subjects to study, a lot of home tasks and plenty of other interesting activities. If you don`t want to know absolutely everything about every subject, I`ll recommend you to refuse from going on some lecturies and activities. But you need to be accurate with it. It`s easy to start getting bad marks, so you need to decide what lecturies you will be visiting and what tasks will be doind.

I hope me suggestions will help you, good luck and good bye.","expert_annotation":{"annotator_id":8,"errors":[{"text":"in studing","labels":["Grammar","Spelling"],"start":66,"end":76},{"text":"in university.","labels":["Grammar"],"start":77,"end":91},{"text":"in the beggining","labels":["Grammar","Lexical choice"],"start":173,"end":189},{"text":"studing","labels":["Spelling"],"start":198,"end":205},{"text":"around","labels":["Grammar"],"start":218,"end":224},{"text":" new and ","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":227,"end":236},{"text":"need a time","labels":["Grammar"],"start":245,"end":256},{"text":"take some time from you","labels":["Lexical choice"],"start":340,"end":363},{"text":"studying and ","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":411,"end":424},{"text":"studing","labels":["Spelling"],"start":516,"end":523},{"text":"manegement","labels":["Spelling"],"start":574,"end":584},{"text":"university","labels":["Grammar"],"start":592,"end":602},{"text":"home tasks","labels":["Lexical choice"],"start":640,"end":650},{"text":"I`ll recommend you to refuse","labels":["Grammar"],"start":764,"end":792},{"text":"refuse from going","labels":["Grammar"],"start":786,"end":803},{"text":"going on some lecturies","labels":["Lexical choice"],"start":798,"end":821},{"text":"lecturies","labels":["Spelling"],"start":812,"end":821},{"text":"accurate","labels":["Lexical choice"],"start":857,"end":865},{"text":"lecturies","labels":["Spelling"],"start":940,"end":949},{"text":"doind","labels":["Spelling"],"start":994,"end":999},{"text":"me","labels":["Grammar"],"start":1009,"end":1011}],"strengths":[{"text":"The second thing","labels":["Sentence structure ✓"],"start":549,"end":565}],"comments":[]}},{"ref_id":"sample_07013","cefr_level":"A2","task":"You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.","student_text":"Hi, im styduing in the MIPT - in one of the hardest technical universitiyes in Russia, and i want to give you some advices.

My first advidce - it to sleep from 7 to 9 hours (how much exactly depends from you) in day, and you should stand from up and go to bed in one time in day. With sleeping regulary in one time you will feel better and maybe you will feel better.

Next advice - it to make calendar with notions, or something like this, it helps to plan day wery succesfull. Sometimes procrastination wins you, but with notions it weel be easier to make hometasks in time.

The third advice - food delivery, if you have not enought time to cook something, or to go somewhere in big sity you can easily get food from delivery services. But sometimes people eats only food from delivery, and even don't go out from their rooms, of course it isn't good.

And the last what i want to say - probably your biggest troubles with time it that you have not hard character. Without this super ability it hard to self-controle, may be you should to ask your friends wathching what are you doing in free time, they can anounce if you spend time on games or other time-killers.

I believe that my advices can help to had more free time, good bye.","expert_annotation":{"annotator_id":5,"errors":[{"text":"im","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":4,"end":6},{"text":"styduing","labels":["Spelling"],"start":7,"end":15},{"text":"in the MIPT","labels":["Grammar"],"start":16,"end":27},{"text":" in","labels":["Grammar"],"start":29,"end":32},{"text":"i","labels":["Punctuation"],"start":91,"end":92},{"text":"some advices","labels":["Grammar"],"start":110,"end":122},{"text":"advidce","labels":["Grammar"],"start":134,"end":141},{"text":"advidce","labels":["Spelling"],"start":134,"end":141},{"text":"it to sleep","labels":["Grammar","Spelling"],"start":144,"end":155},{"text":"depends from","labels":["Grammar","Spelling"],"start":192,"end":204},{"text":"in day","labels":["Grammar"],"start":210,"end":216},{"text":"stand from up","labels":["Grammar"],"start":233,"end":246},{"text":"","labels":["Grammar","Lexical choice"],"start":261,"end":261},{"text":" in day.
